34	const npGenericKubeproxyIptables = "GenericKubeproxyIptables"
35	
36	var _ = Describe("Event Registry", func() {
37		When("handlers with various network plugins are added to the registry", func() {
38			var (
39				matchingHandlers    []*testing.TestHandler
40				nonMatchingHandlers []*testing.TestHandler
41				registry            *event.Registry
42				allTestEvents       chan testing.TestEvent
43			)
44	
45			BeforeEach(func() {
46				allTestEvents = make(chan testing.TestEvent, 1000)
47	
48				nonMatchingHandlers = []*testing.TestHandler{
49					testing.NewTestHandler("ovn-handler", cni.OVNKubernetes, allTestEvents),
50				}
51	
52				matchingHandlers = []*testing.TestHandler{
53					testing.NewTestHandler("kubeproxy-handler1", npGenericKubeproxyIptables, allTestEvents),
54					testing.NewTestHandler("kubeproxy-handler2", npGenericKubeproxyIptables, allTestEvents),
55					testing.NewTestHandler("wildcard-handler", event.AnyNetworkPlugin, allTestEvents),
56				}
57	
58				var err error
59	
60				registry, err = event.NewRegistry(context.TODO(), "test-registry", npGenericKubeproxyIptables, logger.NewHandler(),
61					matchingHandlers[0], nonMatchingHandlers[0], matchingHandlers[1], matchingHandlers[2])
62				Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
63				Expect(registry.GetName()).To(Equal("test-registry"))
64			})
65	
66			It("should initialize all matching handlers", func() {
67				for _, h := range matchingHandlers {
68					Expect(h.Initialized).To(BeTrue())
69				}
70	
71				for _, h := range nonMatchingHandlers {
72					Expect(h.Initialized).To(BeFalse())
73				}
74			})
75	
76			It("should invoke all matching handlers of all events in registration order", func() {
77				for ev, f := range allEvents(registry) {
78					err := f()
79					Expect(err).To(Succeed())
80	
81					for _, h := range matchingHandlers {
82						ev.Handler = h.Name
83						Expect(allTestEvents).To(Receive(Equal(ev)))
84					}
85				}
86	
87				Expect(allTestEvents).ToNot(Receive())
88			})
89	
90			When("one handler returns an error", func() {
91				It("should invoke subsequent matching handlers", func() {
92					events := allEvents(registry)
93					for ev := range events {
94						matchingHandlers[0].FailOnEvent(ev.Name)
95					}
96	
97					for ev, f := range events {
98						err := f()
99						Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred())
100	
101						for i := 1; i < len(matchingHandlers); i++ {
102							ev.Handler = matchingHandlers[i].Name
103							Expect(allTestEvents).To(Receive(Equal(ev)))
104						}
105					}
106				})
107			})
108	
109			Specify("GetHandlers should return all the handlers", func() {
110				handlers := registry.GetHandlers()
111				Expect(handlers).To(ContainElements(matchingHandlers))
112			})
113		})
114	
115		When("a handler's Init fails", func() {
116			It("NewRegistry should return an error", func() {
117				h := testing.NewTestHandler("test-handler", event.AnyNetworkPlugin, make(chan testing.TestEvent, 10))
118				h.FailOnEvent(testing.EvInit)
119	
120				_, err := event.NewRegistry(context.TODO(), "test-registry", event.AnyNetworkPlugin, h)
121				Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred())
122			})
123		})
124	})
125	
126	var _ = Describe("Event Handler", func() {
127		When("SetState has not been called", func() {
128			Specify("State should return a default instance", func() {
129				h := testing.NewTestHandler("test", event.AnyNetworkPlugin, nil)
130				state := h.State()
131				Expect(state).ToNot(BeNil())
132				Expect(state.IsOnGateway()).To(BeFalse())
133				Expect(state.GetRemoteEndpoints()).To(BeEmpty())
134			})
135		})
136	
137		When("SetState has been called", func() {
138			Specify("State should return the instance", func() {
139				h := testing.NewTestHandler("test", event.AnyNetworkPlugin, nil)
140				state := &testing.TestHandlerState{}
141				h.SetState(state)
142				Expect(h.State()).To(BeIdenticalTo(state))
143			})
144		})
145	
146		Specify("HandlerBase should stub all Endpoint methods", func() {
147			h := event.HandlerBase{}
148			Expect(h.LocalEndpointCreated(&submv1.Endpoint{})).To(Succeed())
149			Expect(h.LocalEndpointUpdated(&submv1.Endpoint{})).To(Succeed())
150			Expect(h.LocalEndpointRemoved(&submv1.Endpoint{})).To(Succeed())
151			Expect(h.RemoteEndpointCreated(&submv1.Endpoint{})).To(Succeed())
152			Expect(h.RemoteEndpointUpdated(&submv1.Endpoint{})).To(Succeed())
153			Expect(h.RemoteEndpointRemoved(&submv1.Endpoint{})).To(Succeed())
154			Expect(h.TransitionToGateway()).To(Succeed())
155			Expect(h.TransitionToNonGateway()).To(Succeed())
156		})
157	
158		Specify("NodeHandlerBase should stub all Node methods", func() {
159			h := event.NodeHandlerBase{}
160			Expect(h.NodeCreated(&corev1.Node{})).To(Succeed())
161			Expect(h.NodeUpdated(&corev1.Node{})).To(Succeed())
162			Expect(h.NodeRemoved(&corev1.Node{})).To(Succeed())

167		return map[testing.TestEvent]func() error{
168			{Name: testing.EvStop}:      func() error { return registry.StopHandlers() },
169			{Name: testing.EvUninstall}: func() error { return registry.Uninstall() },
170		}

18	
19	package controller
20	
21	import (
22		"fmt"
23		"os"
24		"sync"
25		"sync/atomic"
26	
27		"github.com/kelseyhightower/envconfig"
28		"github.com/pkg/errors"
29		"github.com/submariner-io/admiral/pkg/federate"
30		"github.com/submariner-io/admiral/pkg/log"
31		"github.com/submariner-io/admiral/pkg/syncer"
32		subv1 "github.com/submariner-io/submariner/pkg/apis/submariner.io/v1"
33		"github.com/submariner-io/submariner/pkg/event"
34		k8sv1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
35		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta"
36		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
37		"k8s.io/client-go/dynamic"
38		"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache"
39		logf "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log"
40	)
41	
42	type specification struct {
43		ClusterID string
44		Namespace string
45	}
46	
47	type handlerStateImpl struct {
48		isOnGateway     atomic.Bool
49		wasOnGateway    bool
50		remoteEndpoints sync.Map
51	}
52	
53	func (s *handlerStateImpl) setIsOnGateway(v bool) {
54		s.isOnGateway.Store(v)
55	}
56	
57	func (s *handlerStateImpl) IsOnGateway() bool {
58		return s.isOnGateway.Load()
59	}
60	
61	func (s *handlerStateImpl) GetRemoteEndpoints() []subv1.Endpoint {
62		var endpoints []subv1.Endpoint
63	
64		s.remoteEndpoints.Range(func(_, value any) bool {
65			endpoints = append(endpoints, *value.(*subv1.Endpoint))
66			return true
67		})
68	
69		return endpoints
70	}
71	
72	type Controller struct {
73		endpointInformer cache.SharedInformer
74		nodeInformer     cache.SharedInformer
75		syncers          []syncer.Interface
76		handlers         *event.Registry
77	}
78	
79	type handlerController struct {
80		syncMutex          sync.Mutex
81		handlerState       handlerStateImpl
82		handler            event.Handler
83		nodeHandler        event.NodeHandler
84		hostname           string
85		clusterID          string
86		lastRemoteEndpoint map[string]*subv1.Endpoint
87	}
88	
89	// If the handler cannot recover from a failure, even after retrying for maximum requeue attempts,
90	// it's best to disregard the event. This prevents the logs from being flooded with repetitive errors.
91	const maxRequeues = 20
92	
93	type Config struct {
94		// Registry is the event handler registry where controller events will be sent.
95		Registry   *event.Registry
96		RestMapper meta.RESTMapper
97		Client     dynamic.Interface
98		Scheme     *runtime.Scheme
99	}
100	
101	var logger = log.Logger{Logger: logf.Log.WithName("EventController")}
102	
103	func New(config *Config) (*Controller, error) {
104		hostname, err := os.Hostname()
105		if err != nil {
106			return nil, errors.Wrapf(err, "unable to read hostname")
107		}
108	
109		ctl := Controller{
110			handlers: config.Registry,
111		}
112	
113		var env specification
114	
115		err = envconfig.Process("submariner", &env)
116		if err != nil {
117			return nil, errors.Wrap(err, "error processing env vars")
118		}
119	
120		syncerConfig := &syncer.ResourceSyncerConfig{
121			SourceNamespace: env.Namespace,
122			SourceClient:    config.Client,
123			RestMapper:      config.RestMapper,
124			Federator:       federate.NewNoopFederator(),
125			ResourceType:    &subv1.Endpoint{},
126		}
127	
128		ctl.endpointInformer, err = syncer.NewSharedInformer(syncerConfig)
129		if err != nil {
130			return nil, errors.Wrap(err, "error creating endpoint informer")
131		}
132	
133		for _, handler := range config.Registry.GetHandlers() {
134			err = ctl.setupHandlerController(handler, env.ClusterID, hostname, *syncerConfig)
135			if err != nil {
136				return nil, err
137			}
138		}
139	
140		return &ctl, nil
141	}
142	
143	// Start starts the controller.
144	func (c *Controller) Start(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error {
145		logger.Info("Starting the Event controller...")
146	
147		runInformer(c.endpointInformer, stopCh)
148		runInformer(c.nodeInformer, stopCh)
149	
150		for _, s := range c.syncers {
151			err := s.Start(stopCh)
152			if err != nil {
153				return errors.Wrap(err, "error starting resource syncer")
154			}
155		}
156	
157		logger.Info("Event controller started")
158	
159		return nil
160	}
161	
162	func (c *Controller) Stop() {
163		logger.Info("Event controller stopping")
164	
165		if err := c.handlers.StopHandlers(); err != nil {
166			logger.Warningf("In Event Controller, StopHandlers returned error: %v", err)
167		}
168	}
169	
170	//nolint:gocritic // Ignore hugeParam - intentional copy.
171	func (c *Controller) setupHandlerController(handler event.Handler, clusterID, hostname string,
172		syncerConfig syncer.ResourceSyncerConfig,
173	) error {
174		hCtrl := &handlerController{
175			handler:            handler,
176			hostname:           hostname,
177			clusterID:          clusterID,
178			lastRemoteEndpoint: map[string]*subv1.Endpoint{},
179		}
180	
181		handler.SetState(&hCtrl.handlerState)
182	
183		syncerConfig.Name = fmt.Sprintf("Endpoint watcher for handler %q", handler.GetName())
184	
185		syncerConfig.Transform = func(obj runtime.Object, numRequeues int, op syncer.Operation) (runtime.Object, bool) {
186			switch op {
187			case syncer.Create:
188				return nil, hCtrl.handleCreatedEndpoint(obj.(*subv1.Endpoint), numRequeues)
189			case syncer.Update:
190				return nil, hCtrl.handleUpdatedEndpoint(obj.(*subv1.Endpoint), numRequeues)
191			case syncer.Delete:
192				return nil, hCtrl.handleRemovedEndpoint(obj.(*subv1.Endpoint), numRequeues)
193			}
194	
195			return nil, false
196		}
197	
198		endpointSyncer, err := syncer.NewResourceSyncerWithSharedInformer(&syncerConfig, c.endpointInformer)
199		if err != nil {
200			return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating resource syncer")
201		}
202	
203		c.syncers = append(c.syncers, endpointSyncer)
204	
205		var ok bool
206	
207		hCtrl.nodeHandler, ok = hCtrl.handler.(event.NodeHandler)
208		if ok {
209			syncerConfig.Name = fmt.Sprintf("Node watcher for handler %q", handler.GetName())
210			syncerConfig.ResourceType = &k8sv1.Node{}
211			syncerConfig.SourceNamespace = k8sv1.NamespaceAll
212	
213			if c.nodeInformer == nil {
214				c.nodeInformer, err = syncer.NewSharedInformer(&syncerConfig)
215				if err != nil {
216					return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating node informer")
217				}
218			}
219	
220			syncerConfig.Transform = func(obj runtime.Object, numRequeues int, op syncer.Operation) (runtime.Object, bool) {
221				switch op {
222				case syncer.Create:
223					return nil, hCtrl.handleCreatedNode(obj.(*k8sv1.Node), numRequeues)
224				case syncer.Update:
225					return nil, hCtrl.handleUpdatedNode(obj.(*k8sv1.Node), numRequeues)
226				case syncer.Delete:
227					return nil, hCtrl.handleRemovedNode(obj.(*k8sv1.Node), numRequeues)
228				}
229	
230				return nil, false
231			}
232	
233			nodeSyncer, err := syncer.NewResourceSyncerWithSharedInformer(&syncerConfig, c.nodeInformer)
234			if err != nil {
235				return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating resource syncer")
236			}
237	
238			c.syncers = append(c.syncers, nodeSyncer)
239		}
240	
241		return nil
242	}

33	func (kp *SyncHandler) Uninstall() error {
34		logger.Infof("Uninstalling Submariner changes from the node %q", kp.hostname)
35		logger.Infof("Flushing route table %d entries", constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID)
36	
37		err := kp.netLink.FlushRouteTable(constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID)
38		if err != nil {
39			// We can safely ignore this error, as this table will exist only on GW nodes
40			logger.V(log.TRACE).Infof("Flushing routing table %d returned error. Can be ignored on non-Gw node: %v",
41				constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID, err)
42		}
43	
44		err = kp.netLink.RuleDelIfPresent(netlinkAPI.NewTableRule(constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID, kp.ipFamily))
45		if err != nil {
46			logger.V(log.TRACE).Infof("Deleting IP Rule pointing to %d table returned error: %v",
47				constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID, err)
48		}
49	
50		kp.deleteVxLANInterface()
51		kp.deleteIPTableChains()
52	
53		return nil
54	}

57		iface := &netlink.Vxlan{

60				Flags: net.FlagUp,
61			},
62			VxlanId: 100,
63			SrcAddr: nil,
64			Port:    port.IntraClusterVxLAN,
65		}
66	
67		logger.Infof("Deleting the %q interface", kp.vxlanIface)
68	
69		err := netlinkAPI.New().LinkDel(iface)
70		if err != nil {
71			logger.Errorf(err, "Failed to delete the vxlan interface %q", kp.vxlanIface)

75	func (kp *SyncHandler) deleteIPTableChains() {
76		pFilter, err := packetfilter.New(kp.ipFamily)
77		if err != nil {
78			logger.Errorf(err, "Failed to initialize packetfilter interface")
79			return
80		}
81	
82		logger.Infof("Flushing IPv%v packetfilter entries in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmPostRouting, constants.NATTable)
83	
84		if err := pFilter.ClearChain(packetfilter.TableTypeNAT, chains.SmPostRouting); err != nil {
85			logger.Errorf(err, "Error flushing packetfilter chain %q of %q table", chains.SmPostRouting,
86				constants.NATTable)
87		}
88	
89		logger.Infof("Deleting IPv%v packetfilter entry in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmPostRouting, constants.NATTable)
90	
91		chain := packetfilter.ChainIPHook{
92			Name:     chains.SmPostRouting,
93			Type:     packetfilter.ChainTypeNAT,
94			Hook:     packetfilter.ChainHookPostrouting,
95			Priority: packetfilter.ChainPriorityFirst,
96		}
97		if err := pFilter.DeleteIPHookChain(&chain); err != nil {
98			logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting IPv%v IPHook chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmPostRouting,
99				constants.NATTable)
100		}
101	
102		logger.Infof("Flushing IPv%v packetfilter entries in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmInput, constants.FilterTable)
103	
104		if err := pFilter.ClearChain(packetfilter.TableTypeFilter, chains.SmInput); err != nil {
105			logger.Errorf(err, "Error flushing IPv%v packetfilter chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmInput,
106				constants.FilterTable)
107		}
108	
109		logger.Infof("Deleting IPv%v packetfilter entry in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmInput, constants.FilterTable)
110	
111		chain = packetfilter.ChainIPHook{
112			Name:     chains.SmInput,
113			Type:     packetfilter.ChainTypeFilter,
114			Hook:     packetfilter.ChainHookInput,
115			Priority: packetfilter.ChainPriorityLast,
116			JumpRule: &packetfilter.Rule{
117				Proto:       packetfilter.RuleProtoUDP,
118				Action:      packetfilter.RuleActionJump,
119				TargetChain: chains.SmInput,
120			},
121		}
122		if err := pFilter.DeleteIPHookChain(&chain); err != nil {
123			logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting IPv%v IPHook chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmInput,
124				constants.FilterTable)
125		}
126	
127		logger.Infof("Flushing IPv%v packetfilter entries in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmSelfSnat, constants.NATTable)
128	
129		if err := pFilter.ClearChain(packetfilter.TableTypeNAT, chains.SmSelfSnat); err != nil {
130			logger.Errorf(err, "Error flushing IPv%v packetfilter chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmSelfSnat,
131				constants.NATTable)
132		}
133	
134		chain = packetfilter.ChainIPHook{
135			Name:     chains.SmSelfSnat,
136			Type:     packetfilter.ChainTypeNAT,
137			Hook:     packetfilter.ChainHookPostrouting,
138			Priority: packetfilter.ChainPriorityMiddle,
139		}
140	
141		if err := pFilter.DeleteIPHookChain(&chain); err != nil {
142			logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting IPv%v IPHook chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmSelfSnat,
143				constants.NATTable)
144		}
145	
146		logger.Infof("Flushing IPv%v packetfilter entries in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmForward, constants.FilterTable)
147	
148		if err := pFilter.ClearChain(packetfilter.TableTypeFilter, chains.SmForward); err != nil {
149			logger.Errorf(err, "Error flushing IPv%v packetfilter chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmForward,
150				constants.FilterTable)
151		}
152	
153		chain = packetfilter.ChainIPHook{
154			Name:     chains.SmForward,
155			Type:     packetfilter.ChainTypeFilter,
156			Hook:     packetfilter.ChainHookForward,
157			Priority: packetfilter.ChainPriorityFirst,

161			logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting IPv%v IPHook chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmForward,
162				constants.FilterTable)

167	func (h *calicoIPPoolHandler) createIPPool(endpoint *submV1.Endpoint) error {
168		subnets := cidr.ExtractSubnets(k8snet.IPv4, endpoint.Spec.Subnets)
169		var retErrors []error
170	
171		for _, subnet := range subnets {
172			iPPoolObj := &calicoapi.IPPool{
173				ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
174					Name:   getEndpointSubnetIPPoolName(endpoint, subnet),
175					Labels: map[string]string{SubmarinerIPPool: "true"},
176				},
177				Spec: calicoapi.IPPoolSpec{
178					CIDR:             subnet,
179					NATOutgoing:      false,
180					Disabled:         true,
181					DisableBGPExport: true,
182				},
183			}
184	
185			_, err := h.client.ProjectcalicoV3().IPPools().Create(context.TODO(), iPPoolObj, metav1.CreateOptions{})
186			if err == nil {
187				logger.Infof("Successfully created Calico IPPool %q", iPPoolObj.GetName())
188				continue
189			}
190	
191			if !apierrors.IsAlreadyExists(err) {
192				retErrors = append(retErrors,
193					errors.Wrapf(err, "error creating Calico IPPool for ClusterID %q subnet %q (is Calico API server running?)",
194						endpoint.Spec.ClusterID, subnet))
195			}
196		}
197	
198		return goerrors.Join(retErrors...)
199	}

211				logger.Infof("Successfully deleted Calico IPPool %q", poolName)

215			if !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
216				retErrors = append(retErrors,
217					errors.Wrapf(err, "error deleting Calico IPPool for ClusterID %q subnet %q (is Calico API server running?)",
218						endpoint.Spec.ClusterID, subnet))

232			Resource(corev1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource("services")).
233			Namespace(h.namespace).
234			Get(ctx, GwLBSvcName, metav1.GetOptions{})
235		if apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
236			return false, nil
237		}

239		if err != nil {
240			return false, errors.Wrap(err, "error reading gw lb service")
241		}
242	
243		return serviceUnstructured.GetAnnotations()[GwLBSvcROKSAnnotation] != "", nil

246	// workaround to address datapath issue with default Calico IPPool configuration for ROKS platform,
247	// IPIPMode of calico default IPPool should be set to 'Always'.
248	func (h *calicoIPPoolHandler) updateROKSCalicoCfg(ctx context.Context) error {
249		isROKS, err := h.platformIsROKS(ctx)
250		if err != nil {
251			return err

253	
254		if !isROKS {
255			return nil

258		// platform is ROKS, make sure that encapsulation of default Installation is set to IPIP
259		// In this way the Tigera Operator will set IPIPMode of default IPPool to Always
260		err = util.Update(ctx, resource.ForDynamic(h.dynClient.Resource(InstallationsGVR)),
261			resource.MustToUnstructured(&tigerav1.Installation{
262				ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
263					Name: DefaultInstallationName,
264				},
265			}), func(existing *unstructured.Unstructured) (*unstructured.Unstructured, error) {
266				installation := &tigerav1.Installation{}
267				err := runtime.DefaultUnstructuredConverter.FromUnstructured(existing.Object, installation)
268				utilruntime.Must(err)
269	
270				if installation.Spec.CalicoNetwork == nil {
271					installation.Spec.CalicoNetwork = &tigerav1.CalicoNetworkSpec{}
272				}
273	
274				ipPools := installation.Spec.CalicoNetwork.IPPools
275				if len(ipPools) > 0 && ipPools[0].Encapsulation == tigerav1.EncapsulationIPIP {
276					logger.Infof("Encapsulation of %s Installation already set to IPIP", DefaultInstallationName)
277					return existing, nil
278				}
279	
280				if len(ipPools) == 0 {
281					logger.Infof("IPPools is empty, so nothing changed")
282					return existing, nil
283				}
284	
285				if installation.Annotations == nil {
286					installation.Annotations = map[string]string{}
287				}
288	
289				installation.Annotations[submarinerPrevEncapsulation] = ipPools[0].Encapsulation.String()
290				installation.Annotations[submarinerManagedLabel] = "true"
291	
292				ipPools[0].Encapsulation = tigerav1.EncapsulationIPIP
293	
294				return resource.MustToUnstructured(installation), nil
295			})
296	
297		return errors.Wrapf(err, "failed to update Installation %q", DefaultInstallationName)
298	}
299	
300	func (h *calicoIPPoolHandler) restoreROKSCalicoCfg() error {
301		err := util.Update(context.TODO(), resource.ForDynamic(h.dynClient.Resource(InstallationsGVR)),
302			resource.MustToUnstructured(&tigerav1.Installation{
303				ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
304					Name: DefaultInstallationName,
305				},
306			}), func(existing *unstructured.Unstructured) (*unstructured.Unstructured, error) {
307				installation := &tigerav1.Installation{}
308				err := runtime.DefaultUnstructuredConverter.FromUnstructured(existing.Object, installation)
309				utilruntime.Must(err)
310	
311				prevEncapsulation := installation.Annotations[submarinerPrevEncapsulation]
312				if prevEncapsulation == "" {
313					return existing, nil // no need to update
314				}
315	
316				ipPools := installation.Spec.CalicoNetwork.IPPools
317				if len(ipPools) > 0 {
318					ipPools[0].Encapsulation = tigerav1.EncapsulationType(prevEncapsulation)
319				}
320	
321				delete(installation.Labels, submarinerManagedLabel)
322				delete(installation.Annotations, submarinerPrevEncapsulation)
323	
324				return resource.MustToUnstructured(installation), nil
325			})
326	
327		return errors.Wrapf(err, "failed to restore ROKS Calico config for Installation %q", DefaultInstallationName)
328	}

40	type forceMssSts int
41	
42	const (
43		notNeeded forceMssSts = iota
44		needed
45		configured
46	)
47	
48	const (
49		// MaxIPSecOverhead - TCP MSS = Default_Iface_MTU - TCP_H(20)-IP_H(20)-max_IpsecOverhed(80).
50		MaxIPSecOverhead    = 120
51		RemoteCIDRIPSetIPv4 = "SUBMARINER-REMOTECIDRS"
52		LocalCIDRIPSetIPv4  = "SUBMARINER-LOCALCIDRS"
53		RemoteCIDRIPSetIPv6 = "SUBMARINER-REMOTECIDRS-V6"
54		LocalCIDRIPSetIPv6  = "SUBMARINER-LOCALCIDRS-V6"
55	)

57	type mtuHandler struct {
58		event.HandlerBase
59		ipFamily         k8snet.IPFamily
60		localClusterCidr []string
61		pFilter          packetfilter.Interface
62		tableType        packetfilter.TableType
63		chainType        packetfilter.ChainType
64		remoteIPSet      packetfilter.NamedSet
65		localIPSet       packetfilter.NamedSet
66		forceMss         forceMssSts
67		tcpMssValue      int
68		localCIDRIPSet   string
69		remoteCIDRIPSet  string
70	}

74	func NewHandler(ipFamily k8snet.IPFamily, localClusterCidr []string, isGlobalnet bool, tcpMssValue int) event.Handler {
75		forceMss := notNeeded
76		if isGlobalnet || tcpMssValue != 0 {
77			forceMss = needed
78		}
79	
80		h := &mtuHandler{
81			ipFamily:         ipFamily,
82			localClusterCidr: cidr.ExtractSubnets(ipFamily, localClusterCidr),
83			forceMss:         forceMss,
84			tcpMssValue:      tcpMssValue,
85			localCIDRIPSet:   LocalCIDRIPSetIPv4,
86			remoteCIDRIPSet:  RemoteCIDRIPSetIPv4,
87		}
88	
89		if ipFamily == k8snet.IPv6 {
90			h.localCIDRIPSet = LocalCIDRIPSetIPv6
91			h.remoteCIDRIPSet = RemoteCIDRIPSetIPv6
92		}
93	
94		return h
95	}

97	func (h *mtuHandler) GetNetworkPlugins() []string {
98		return []string{event.AnyNetworkPlugin}
99	}
100	
101	func (h *mtuHandler) GetName() string {
102		return fmt.Sprintf("MTU handler IPv%s", h.ipFamily)
103	}

105	func (h *mtuHandler) Init(_ context.Context) error {
106		var err error
107	
108		h.pFilter, err = packetfilter.New(h.ipFamily)
109		if err != nil {
110			return errors.Wrap(err, "error initializing iptables")
111		}
112	
113		h.tableType, h.chainType = h.pFilter.GetMSSClampTypes()
114	
115		if err := h.pFilter.CreateIPHookChainIfNotExists(&packetfilter.ChainIPHook{
116			Name:     chains.SmPostRoutingMss,
117			Type:     h.chainType,
118			Hook:     packetfilter.ChainHookPostrouting,
119			Priority: packetfilter.ChainPriorityFirst,
120		}); err != nil {
121			return errors.Wrapf(err, "error creating IPHookChain chain %s", chains.SmPostRoutingMss)
122		}
123	
124		h.remoteIPSet = h.newNamedSetSet(h.remoteCIDRIPSet)
125		if err := h.remoteIPSet.Create(true); err != nil {
126			return errors.Wrapf(err, "error creating ipset %q", h.remoteCIDRIPSet)
127		}
128	
129		h.localIPSet = h.newNamedSetSet(h.localCIDRIPSet)
130		if err := h.localIPSet.Create(true); err != nil {
131			return errors.Wrapf(err, "error creating ipset %q", h.localCIDRIPSet)
132		}
133	
134		// packetfilter rules to clamp TCP MSS to a fixed value will be programmed when the local endpoint is created
135		if h.forceMss == needed {
136			return nil
137		}
138	
139		logger.Info("Creating packetfilter clamp-mss-to-pmtu rules")
140	
141		ruleSpecSource := &packetfilter.Rule{
142			SrcSetName:  h.localCIDRIPSet,
143			DestSetName: h.remoteCIDRIPSet,
144			Action:      packetfilter.RuleActionMss,
145			ClampType:   packetfilter.ToPMTU,
146		}
147	
148		ruleSpecDest := &packetfilter.Rule{
149			SrcSetName:  h.remoteCIDRIPSet,
150			DestSetName: h.localCIDRIPSet,
151			Action:      packetfilter.RuleActionMss,
152			ClampType:   packetfilter.ToPMTU,
153		}
154	
155		if err := h.pFilter.AppendUnique(h.tableType, chains.SmPostRoutingMss, ruleSpecSource); err != nil {
156			return errors.Wrapf(err, "error appending packetfilter rule %q", ruleSpecSource)
157		}
158	
159		if err := h.pFilter.AppendUnique(h.tableType, chains.SmPostRoutingMss, ruleSpecDest); err != nil {
160			return errors.Wrapf(err, "error appending packetfilter rule %q", ruleSpecDest)
161		}
162	
163		return nil
164	}
165	
166	func (h *mtuHandler) LocalEndpointCreated(endpoint *submV1.Endpoint) error {
167		subnets := cidr.ExtractSubnets(h.ipFamily, endpoint.Spec.Subnets)
168		for _, subnet := range subnets {
169			err := h.localIPSet.AddEntry(subnet, true)
170			if err != nil {
171				return errors.Wrap(err, "error adding local IP set entry")
172			}
173		}
174	
175		for _, subnet := range h.localClusterCidr {
176			err := h.localIPSet.AddEntry(subnet, true)
177			if err != nil {
178				return errors.Wrap(err, "error adding localClusterCidr IP set entry")
179			}
180		}
181	
182		if h.forceMss == needed {
183			logger.Info("Creating packetfilter set-mss rules")
184	
185			err := h.forceMssClamping(endpoint)
186			if err != nil {
187				return errors.Wrap(err, "error forcing TCP MSS clamping")
188			}
189	
190			h.forceMss = configured
191		}
192	
193		return nil
194	}
195	
196	func (h *mtuHandler) LocalEndpointRemoved(endpoint *submV1.Endpoint) error {
197		subnets := cidr.ExtractSubnets(h.ipFamily, endpoint.Spec.Subnets)
198		for _, subnet := range subnets {
199			logError(h.localIPSet.DelEntry(subnet), "Error deleting the subnet %q from the local IPSet", subnet)
200		}
201	
202		for _, subnet := range h.localClusterCidr {
203			logError(h.localIPSet.DelEntry(subnet), "Error deleting the subnet %q from the local IPSet", subnet)
204		}
205	
206		return nil
207	}

209	func (h *mtuHandler) RemoteEndpointCreated(endpoint *submV1.Endpoint) error {
210		logger.Infof("Processing created Endpoint %q", endpoint.Spec.CableName)
211	
212		subnets := cidr.ExtractSubnets(h.ipFamily, endpoint.Spec.Subnets)
213		for _, subnet := range subnets {
214			err := h.remoteIPSet.AddEntry(subnet, true)
215			if err != nil {
216				return errors.Wrap(err, "error adding remote IP set entry")
217			}
218		}
219	
220		return nil
221	}

223	func (h *mtuHandler) RemoteEndpointRemoved(endpoint *submV1.Endpoint) error {
224		logger.Infof("Processing removed Endpoint %q", endpoint.Spec.CableName)
225	
226		subnets := cidr.ExtractSubnets(h.ipFamily, endpoint.Spec.Subnets)
227		for _, subnet := range subnets {
228			logError(h.remoteIPSet.DelEntry(subnet), "Error deleting the subnet %q from the remote IPSet", subnet)
229		}
230	
231		return nil
232	}

234	func (h *mtuHandler) newNamedSetSet(key string) packetfilter.NamedSet {
235		return h.pFilter.NewNamedSet(&packetfilter.SetInfo{
236			Name: key,
237		})
238	}

240	func (h *mtuHandler) Uninstall() error {
241		logger.Infof("Flushing packetfilter entries in %q chain of table type %q", chains.SmPostRoutingMss, h.tableType.String())
242	
243		logError(h.pFilter.ClearChain(h.tableType, chains.SmPostRoutingMss),
244			"Error flushing chain %q of table type %q", chains.SmPostRoutingMss, h.tableType.String())
245	
246		logger.Infof("Deleting IPHook chain %q of table type %q", chains.SmPostRoutingMss, h.tableType.String())
247	
248		logError(h.pFilter.DeleteIPHookChain(&packetfilter.ChainIPHook{
249			Name:     chains.SmPostRoutingMss,
250			Type:     h.chainType,
251			Hook:     packetfilter.ChainHookPostrouting,
252			Priority: packetfilter.ChainPriorityFirst,
253		}), "Error deleting IP hook chain %q of table type %q", chains.SmPostRoutingMss, h.tableType.String())
254	
255		logError(h.localIPSet.Flush(), "Error flushing ipset %q", h.localCIDRIPSet)
256	
257		logError(h.localIPSet.Destroy(), "Error deleting ipset %q", h.localCIDRIPSet)
258	
259		logError(h.remoteIPSet.Flush(), "Error flushing ipset %q", h.remoteCIDRIPSet)
260	
261		logError(h.remoteIPSet.Destroy(), "Error deleting ipset %q", h.remoteCIDRIPSet)
262	
263		return nil
264	}
265	
266	func (h *mtuHandler) forceMssClamping(endpoint *submV1.Endpoint) error {
267		tcpMssSrc := "user"
268		tcpMssValue := h.tcpMssValue
269	
270		if tcpMssValue == 0 {
271			defaultHostIface, err := netlinkAPI.New().GetDefaultGatewayInterface(h.ipFamily)
272			if err != nil {
273				return errors.Wrapf(err, "Unable to find the default interface on host")
274			}
275	
276			overHeadSize := MaxIPSecOverhead
277			if endpoint.Spec.Backend == vxlandriver.CableDriverName {
278				overHeadSize = vxlan.MTUOverhead
279			}
280	
281			tcpMssValue = defaultHostIface.MTU() - overHeadSize
282			tcpMssSrc = "default"
283		}
284	
285		logger.Infof("forceMssClamping to: %d (%s) ", tcpMssValue, tcpMssSrc)
286	
287		rules := []*packetfilter.Rule{
288			{
289				SrcSetName:  h.localCIDRIPSet,
290				DestSetName: h.remoteCIDRIPSet,
291				Action:      packetfilter.RuleActionMss,
292				ClampType:   packetfilter.ToValue,
293				MssValue:    strconv.Itoa(tcpMssValue),
294			},
295			{
296				DestSetName: h.localCIDRIPSet,
297				SrcSetName:  h.remoteCIDRIPSet,
298				Action:      packetfilter.RuleActionMss,
299				ClampType:   packetfilter.ToValue,
300				MssValue:    strconv.Itoa(tcpMssValue),
301			},
302		}
303	
304		if err := h.pFilter.UpdateChainRules(h.tableType, chains.SmPostRoutingMss, rules); err != nil {
305			return errors.Wrapf(err, "error updating chain %s table type %q", chains.SmPostRoutingMss, h.tableType.String())
306		}
307	
308		return nil
309	}

311	func logError(err error, format string, args ...any) {
312		if err != nil {
313			logger.Errorf(err, format, args...)
314		}
315	}

The fix is complete. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Summary

The bug was that the `Handler` interface's `Stop()` and `Uninstall()` methods did not accept a `context.Context` parameter, preventing callers from imposing deadlines or cancelling internal operations.

### Changes made (17 files, 38 insertions, 33 deletions):

**Core interface change (`pkg/event/handler.go`):**
- `Handler.Stop()` → `Handler.Stop(ctx context.Context)`
- `Handler.Uninstall()` → `Handler.Uninstall(ctx context.Context)`
- `HandlerBase.Stop()` / `HandlerBase.Uninstall()` updated accordingly

**Registry change (`pkg/event/registry.go`):**
- `Registry.StopHandlers()` → `Registry.StopHandlers(ctx context.Context)` — passes `ctx` to each handler's `Stop()`
- `Registry.Uninstall()` → `Registry.Uninstall(ctx context.Context)` — passes `ctx` to each handler's `Uninstall()`

**Handler implementations updated:**
- `pkg/routeagent_driver/handlers/ovn/uninstall.go` — `Handler.Stop()`, `Handler.Uninstall()`
- `pkg/routeagent_driver/handlers/kubeproxy/uninstall.go` — `SyncHandler.Uninstall()`
- `pkg/routeagent_driver/handlers/calico/ippool_handler.go` — `calicoIPPoolHandler.Uninstall()` (now uses passed `ctx` instead of `context.TODO()`)
- `pkg/routeagent_driver/handlers/healthchecker/healthchecker.go` — `controller.Stop()` (now uses passed `ctx` instead of `context.TODO()`)
- `pkg/routeagent_driver/handlers/mtu/mtuhandler.go` — `mtuHandler.Uninstall()`
- `pkg/globalnet/controllers/gateway_monitor.go` — `gatewayMonitor.Stop()` (now uses passed `ctx` as base for its timeout)
- `pkg/event/testing/testing.go` — `TestHandlerBase.Stop()`, `TestHandlerBase.Uninstall()`

**Callers updated:**
- `pkg/event/controller/controller.go` — passes `context.TODO()` to `StopHandlers()`
- `pkg/routeagent_driver/main.go` — passes `context.TODO()` to `StopHandlers()` and `Uninstall()`
- `pkg/globalnet/controllers/gateway_monitor.go` — passes `context.TODO()` to `gatewayMonitor.Stop()`

**Test files updated:**
- All test call sites updated to pass `context.TODO()`
